description: UE5 string types: FString vs FName vs FText — when to use each, conversion, and common operations

entries.title: FString, FName, FText — When to Use Which
entries.description: UE5 has three string types that serve different purposes. FString is for general manipulation, FName is for fast comparison identifiers, and FText is for user-facing localized text.
entries.whatItIs: UE5's three string types each optimize for a different use case: FString is a mutable, heap-allocated string for general manipulation; FName is an immutable, hash-indexed identifier for fast comparison (used by the engine for asset names, bone names, tags); and FText is a localization-aware string for any text shown to the player.
whenToUse: FString: building strings dynamically, parsing, file paths, debug logging, any string manipulation
FName: identifiers that need fast comparison — bone names, socket names, tags, DataTable row names, map keys
FText: anything the player sees — UI text, item names, dialogue, tooltips, notifications
Rule of thumb: FName for IDs, FText for display, FString for everything else
whenNotToUse: FString for identifiers compared frequently — FName is O(1) comparison vs FString's O(n)
FName for displayed text — FName is case-insensitive and not localizable
FText for internal identifiers — FText has localization overhead that's wasted for non-displayed strings
Raw TCHAR*/char* — always use the UE5 types for proper memory management and platform support
commonMistakes: Using FString for map keys or frequent comparisons — FName is orders of magnitude faster for lookups
Using FName for player-visible text — FName is case-insensitive ("Sword" == "SWORD") and cannot be localized
Forgetting the * when passing FString to UE_LOG: UE_LOG(LogTemp, Log, TEXT("%s"), *MyString) — missing * crashes
Creating FNames at runtime in hot loops — FName construction involves a hash table lookup; cache the FName
Using FString::Printf when FText::Format should be used for UI — FText::Format supports localization and named arguments
Comparing FText with == directly — use FText::EqualTo() or FText::CompareTo() for locale-aware comparison
keyPoints: FString: mutable, heap-allocated. Supports: Printf, Append, Contains, Replace, Split, ToUpper/Lower, Len, IsEmpty
FName: immutable, hash-indexed. Created from string once, compared by hash. Case-insensitive. ~40 bytes.
FText: localization-aware. Created with LOCTEXT, NSLOCTEXT, FText::FromString, or FText::Format.
Conversions: FString → FName: FName(*Str). FName → FString: Name.ToString(). FString → FText: FText::FromString(Str).
FText::Format(LOCTEXT("Fmt", "{0} has {1} HP"), PlayerName, Health): localization-safe formatting with arguments
LOCTEXT("Key", "Text"): creates localizable FText. Requires LOCTEXT_NAMESPACE defined in the file.
TEXT("literal"): creates a TCHAR* literal (not FString, FName, or FText — just raw text for macros)
